name: Rosewood Sand Hill
character: Sixteen acres of orchards, oak groves, and low California-modern bungalows at the base of the Santa Cruz Mountains. Rosewood Sand Hill is the rare luxury hotel that feels like a private estate. It is the unofficial living room of Silicon Valley — Sand Hill Road's venture firms are a five-minute drive — but the property's deepest character is its quiet. Mornings start with fog burning off the hills. The pool is heated year-round. Madera (Michelin-starred) anchors the property. The spa is set in its own building behind the meditation garden. Guests come here to slow down, recover, and think clearly.

cultural_calendar.name: Stanford reunion weekends
cultural_calendar.notes: The property fills with alumni returning to campus. The concierge maintains relationships with Stanford for private campus access and faculty introductions.

cultural_calendar.name: Napa harvest season
cultural_calendar.notes: A 90-minute drive north. The hotel arranges private tastings at family-owned wineries that don't take walk-ins.

cultural_calendar.name: Filoli Gardens bloom
cultural_calendar.notes: Historic estate gardens 15 minutes away. Private after-hours access can be arranged.

cultural_calendar.name: Stanford Symphony / Live at Memorial
cultural_calendar.notes: Classical performances at Stanford's Memorial Church and Bing Concert Hall. The concierge has access to faculty-only seating sections.

signature_amenities: The Spa at Sand Hill (16-acre garden setting)
Sand Hill Trail private morning walks
Curated Napa and Sonoma wine experiences
room_amenity_options: In-room cold-pressed juice and adaptogen options

welcome_amenity_options.name: Napa Valley welcome flight
welcome_amenity_options.description: A small-format tasting of three Napa wines selected by the sommelier, with a card from chef Reylon Agustin. Served in-room or on the terrace.

welcome_amenity_options.name: Hand-blended California herbal tea
welcome_amenity_options.description: A house tea blend from herbs grown in the property's meditation garden. Served with a small ceramic pot, ideal after a long flight.

welcome_amenity_options.name: Recovery basket
welcome_amenity_options.description: Cold-pressed greens, organic dark chocolate, magnesium mineral water, and a handwritten note. For jet-lagged or long-haul arrivals.

welcome_amenity_options.name: Valley essentials
welcome_amenity_options.description: Local sourdough from Manresa Bread, Mariposa Baking shortbread, Equator coffee beans, and seasonal stone fruit from a Half Moon Bay farm.
